title: Architecture interface detail (bundle sub-artifact)
description: Front-matter shape for an architecture-interface-detail artifact. A detail file that belongs to a helicopter Architecture artifact (kind: bundle sub-artifact, Rule 8 in docs/authoring-discipline.md). Lives at <scope>/architecture/interfaces/<NAME>.md when the parent architecture is authored in helicopter+bundle form. Carries the full Design-by-Contract content (preconditions, full postconditions tree, invariants, typed errors, quality_attributes, authentication, authorisation, version, deprecation_policy) that is too verbose for the helicopter interface roster. Tooling treats helicopter + all detail files in the bundle as one logical artifact for graph construction, Spec Ambiguity Test, and review. The id pattern is ARCH-IF-<NAME>; belongs_to points back to the helicopter ARCH(-{scope}) id; subject names the interface the detail describes. Status uses the universal lifecycle (draft | active | superseded). recovery_status applies the same retrofit discipline as the helicopter — observed structure can be reconstructed; the no-fabrication rule is enforced per-field by review skill, not at envelope.

id.description: Detail-file identifier. Pattern: ARCH-IF-<NAME>, where NAME is the interface name (free-form alphanumerics, underscores, and hyphens — covers PascalCase, I-prefixed, snake_case conventions). Globally unique across the spec tree.

artifact_type.description: Artifact-type discriminator, fixed for this schema. Tooling uses this to route to the correct schema; pairs with kind for bundle subtype dispatch.

belongs_to.description: Helicopter artifact ID this detail file belongs to. Must resolve to an architecture artifact in the corpus (TRV-REF-001). The bundle relationship is structural: tooling composes helicopter + detail files into one logical artifact at validation time.

kind.description: Bundle subtype discriminator. Pinned to architecture-interface-detail for this schema; future detail kinds (e.g. architecture-decomposition-detail) will get their own per-kind schemas with matching constants.

subject.description: Interface name this detail file describes. Mirrors the 'name' field on the corresponding interface entry in the helicopter; tooling cross-checks that the helicopter's detail: link resolves to a file whose subject equals the helicopter interface name.

scope.description: Scope path of the helicopter Architecture this detail file belongs to. Mirrors the helicopter's scope; redundant with belongs_to but kept for direct query without graph resolution. Empty string at root scope.

governing_adrs.description: Optional. ADRs that constrain this specific interface beyond the parent architecture's governing_adrs. Most detail files inherit governing ADRs via belongs_to and leave this absent.

status.description: Lifecycle state. Detail files inherit the helicopter's lifecycle in practice — promoting a helicopter to active also promotes its bundle. Tracked separately at schema level for parity with all other artifacts.

interface_detail_body.description: Body-content shape for the YAML block in a detail file's markdown body. Contains the pushed-out Design-by-Contract fields lifted verbatim from the helicopter's interface entry per Rule 8. The schema enforces structural completeness here — the detail file is where DbC completeness lives, not the helicopter. Exported as a $def so parsers and validators can extract the body block directly. Not enforced on the front-matter object.

preconditions.description: What the caller must ensure on the way in. Each item is one precondition expressed as a predicate. Required at the detail level — an interface with no preconditions beyond 'callable' should state that explicitly.

postconditions.description: What the supplier guarantees on the way out, split across outcome (on_success required; on_precondition_failure and on_downstream_failure when distinguished). Same shape as architecture.schema.json#/$defs/interface.contract.postconditions.

invariants.description: Properties that hold across repeat or concurrent calls. See architecture.schema.json#/$defs/interface.contract.invariants.

errors.description: Typed error enumeration. Same shape as architecture.schema.json#/$defs/interface.contract.errors. The 'http' field is required when the protocol has HTTP semantics; for non-HTTP interfaces (in-process call, etc.), the field may be omitted via additionalProperties extension.

quality_attributes.description: NFR budgets the interface carries. Same shape as architecture.schema.json#/$defs/interface.contract.quality_attributes.

authentication.description: Authentication requirement at this interface. Free-form string (e.g. 'mTLS', 'OAuth2 client_credentials', 'n/a — in-process call'). Required content; review skill checks completeness for externally callable interfaces.

authorisation.description: Authorisation requirement at this interface. Free-form string.

version.description: Versioning scheme statement (free-form string), e.g. 'v1 — additive-only-within-major'. Required content; review skill checks completeness.

deprecation_policy.description: Deprecation policy for this interface — minimum notice period, migration path, sunset rules. Free-form string.
